rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TONY AJOUDANI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For A Court Determination Of The Persons Entitled To Distribution Of The Decedents Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to review and detetermine: 1) The form of title controls at death, and Evidence Code section 662 applies instead of the general community property presumption under Family Code section 760 (or section 2581 only applicable when dividing property held in joint form in divorce or legal separation cases). See In re Brace (2020) 9 Cal.5th 903, 934 and Estate of Wall (2021) 68 Cal.App.5th 168, 175. The 2008 transmutation grant deed that added petitioner's name did not specify how the decedent and petitioner intended to hold joint title (e.g., joint tenants or community property), other than as "husband and wife". If petitioner's name had been added before 1975, the community property presumption under Family Code section 803(c) applicable to deeds describing grantees as husband and wife would have controlled, but there is no "husband and wife" title presumption after 1975. While Estate of Bibb (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 461 cited by petitioner (and also in Brace) supports petitioner's position that the 2008 deed operated to transmute the Crestmont Drive property, Bibb involved one spouse's separate property transmuted to both spouses as joint tenants and separate property with rights of survivorship (and not transmuted to spouses as community property without rights of survivorship). i) Should Civil Code section 686 apply to determine the form of title? If so, the decedent and petitioner are deemed to have taken joint title as tenants in common, in which case the decedent and petitioner each held a 50% separate property interest in the Crestmont Drive property. ii) Did petitioner acquire her 50% interest in the Crestmont Drive property from the decedent by gift, despite the "valuable consideration" language recited in the deed? If so, the separate property presumption applicable to marital gifts under Family Code section 770(a)(2) may control, in which case the decedent and petitioner each held a 50% separate property interest in the Crestmont Drive property. iii) Absent any evidence of specific consideration actually given by petitioner in exchange for her 50% community property interest in the Crestmont Drive property, should the Court apply Evidence Code section 1330 to accept the valuable consideration language recited in the deed as sufficient to rebut the separate property gift presumption? iv) Should the Court read Civil Code sections 682(d) and 687 to allow application of the Family Code section 760 community property presumption (see also Probate Code section 28(a)) to treat the "husband and wife" title as community property? 2) No timely election required by Probate Code section 13502(a)(1) was filed by petitioner to voluntarily probate the decedent's property passing to petitioner as the surviving spouse. No court approval has been sought for a late election under section 13502(b), with the statement required by LRSF 14.35(I)(5) to acknowledge other nonprobate options available to petitioner (e.g., a spousal property petition or recording a section 13540 affidavit without any court proceedings). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HURBERT LEE DONALDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. The allegation on the petition seems contradictory as to whether the decedent died testate or intestate. 2. The examiner is unable to complete the review because this petition is missing information; not all sections have been filled out. 3. Additionally, other required documents, such as the original will, notice, proposed order, and duties and liabilities, are not on file. The Court will decide whether to dismiss this petition. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN A. NITIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be addressed at the hearing unless resolved beforehand: (1) Allegation re: addresses being PO Box only of the following heirs at law: Nancy M., Suzanne A., Danny A., and Marcus A., rather than their place of business or personal residence. Prob. Code 1215(a)(3). If they receive mail at the PO Boxes, their relevant affidavits seem necessary. (2) The Court may inquire further information about Madeline Smith's issues as reported in the declaration of due diligence filed on 12/4/25. (3) The Court to determine whether it will require nominations or declinations to acts from other issues of grandparents (Christine C., Stephen C., Denise A., William A., Danny A., and Marcus A.) who are all in the same priority group as those who nominated the SFPA.
